scenario_name: Scenario 16 — Distributed System Failure in Code
justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Courier stuck in reserved state identified as Critical with explicit reproduction steps, (2) All 3 failure modes analyzed — step 2 failure (phantom reservation), step 3 failure (ghost assignment), step 4 failure (silent customer notification loss), (3) Saga without compensation pattern clearly identified and named, (4) Explicit escalation to MJ for saga/architectural design, (5) Specific compensating transaction proposed (courierService.release(courierId)), (6) safe_to_deploy is false.

observations.text: Phantom courier reservation (step 1 success, step 2 failure) identified as Critical with clear reproduction steps and specific fix (courierService.release)
Ghost assignment (step 3 failure) correctly identified as Critical with practical fix options (retry via durable queue or rollback to ASSIGNMENT_PENDING)
Step 4 (customer notification) failure captured as an important issue with appropriate lower severity
Saga pattern explicitly named and escalated to MJ with blocked criteria and recommendation to design compensation before implementation
Went beyond rubric requirements by identifying idempotency/retry risk as an additional critical finding
Suggestions include state machine, async event-driven notifications, and TTL on reservations — all architecturally sound
Confidence section transparently flags assumptions about courier service internals and potential external orchestration
